name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram operates a network of 37+ locations across Albuquerque offering money transfers, check cashing, and bill payment services through partner retailers like 7-Eleven and Walmart.
description_long:
MoneyGram is a money transfer and financial services company that provides convenient access to cash services through a distributed network of retail partnerships rather than branded storefronts. The company has established relationships with major retailers including 7-Eleven convenience stores and Walmart locations, allowing customers to conduct transactions at thousands of points across Albuquerque and nationwide. This partnership model enables MoneyGram to offer services at locations already embedded in residential neighborhoods and shopping areas.

MoneyGram's service offerings focus on money movement and cash access. Customers can send and receive money transfers both online and in-person, cash checks, purchase money orders, make wire transfers, and pay bills through their partner network. The company emphasizes ease of use and accessibility, with services available during extended hours at convenience store locations (many opening at 9:00 AM) and all-day availability at larger retailers like Walmart. The Albuquerque location search shows 37 available service points, with most concentrated at 7-Eleven stores throughout the city.

MoneyGram's primary distinction is its reliance on a third-party retail partner network rather than operating physical branches. This model keeps operating costs low and provides customers with nearby service access integrated into their existing shopping patterns. The company operates both online and offline service channels, allowing flexibility in how customers conduct transfers. The inclusion of multiple Casa De Cambio La Monedita locations suggests strong integration with culturally-focused financial service providers in the Albuquerque market.

The main limitation is that MoneyGram's services are restricted to the availability and hours of its partner retailers. Customers cannot access services at branded MoneyGram locations but must visit 7-Eleven, Walmart, or other partner merchants. Additionally, specific fee structures, transfer limits, and competitive rate information are not disclosed on the location page. As a money transfer service rather than a lender or credit product, MoneyGram does not help customers build credit or access emergency funding.
pros: 37+ service locations throughout Albuquerque for convenient access
Extended hours at 7-Eleven locations, opening as early as 9:00 AM with evening availability
Integrated with major retailers (7-Eleven, Walmart) already in neighborhoods and shopping areas
Both online and offline money transfer options for flexibility
Services available at culturally-focused financial service providers like Casa De Cambio La Monedita
Simple searchable location finder by address, city, state, or ZIP code
Multiple service types in one place (sending, receiving, potentially bill payment)
cons: Service availability dependent on partner retailer hours and locations—no standalone MoneyGram branches
No fee, rate, or transfer limit information disclosed on location pages
Requires visiting a third-party retail location rather than dedicated service center
No credit-building, lending, or emergency loan products offered
Geographic coverage limited to partner merchant networks, potentially unavailable in some neighborhoods
best_for: Customers needing to send or receive money transfers quickly at neighborhood convenience stores
Individuals without traditional bank accounts seeking cash access and money movement services
Spanish-speaking customers in Albuquerque served by Casa De Cambio La Monedita partnership locations
People seeking money services integrated into existing shopping locations like 7-Eleven or Walmart
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for individuals who need to send, receive, or access money quickly through convenient neighborhood retailers and want flexibility between online and in-person services. The main caveat is that services depend entirely on partner retailer availability—there are no dedicated MoneyGram branches, and specific fees and terms are not transparent on their location pages.
services: Offline retail transaction processing at partner locations
